name: Sourdough Starter 1973
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the milk to between 90 and 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Pour it into a clean, 1-1/2 quart glass, plastic, or stainless bowl.
2. Stir in 3 tablespoons of freshly opened low-fat yogurt. Let stand in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
3. After a curd has formed, gradually stir in 1 cup of all-purpose flour. Cover with Saran wrap and let stand for 2 to 5 days in a warm place.
4. This procedure will yield about 1-1/2 cups of starter for each of the servings listed above.
5. Cover and store in the refrigerator. NOTE: If liquid "hooch" turns pink during fermentation, discard and start over with fresh ingredients.
